title: Special Registers
subtitle: {key:"/} {key:":} {key:".} {key:"%} {key:"#} {key:"=} {key:"_} — the read-only and weird ones.
summary: Vim provides several read-only registers that contain useful state: "/ is the last search, ": the last Ex command, ". the last inserted text, "% the current filename, "# the alternate file, "= the expression register, and "_ the black hole.

blocks.text: Beyond the alphabetic and numbered registers, Vim has a small zoo of *special* registers. Most are read-only — Vim writes to them automatically as you work, and you read from them when useful.

rows: Pasting it to edit and re-run
Inserting it in commands or buffer

blocks.title: The Two Most Useful: "_ and "=
blocks.text:
**{key:"}{key:_} (black hole)**. Anything written here disappears. Anything read from it is empty. Use it to delete *without affecting any other register*:

  • {key:"}{key:_}{key:d}{key:d} — delete this line, but don't update unnamed/numbered.
  • {key:"}{key:_}{key:c}{key:i}{key:w} — change this word, but keep the previous yank ready to paste.
  • Visual select something, then {key:"}{key:_}{key:d}{key:p} — replace selection with the unnamed register's content (delete to black hole, then put).

**{key:"}{key:=} (expression)**. In Insert mode, {key:Ctrl-R}{key:=} pops up a `=` prompt. Type any Vim expression, hit {key:Enter}, and the result is inserted as text. Examples:

  • {key:Ctrl-R}{key:=}`5*7+1`{key:Enter} → inserts `36`.
  • {key:Ctrl-R}{key:=}`strftime('%Y-%m-%d')`{key:Enter} → today's date.
  • {key:Ctrl-R}{key:=}`getreg('a')`{key:Enter} → contents of register a (programmatic access).

This is Vim as a calculator inside its editor.
{key:"}{key:%}{key:p} pastes the current filename into the buffer — useful in commit messages, comments, or generated boilerplate. {key:"}{key:#}{key:p} pastes the previous file's name.

{key:"}{key:/}{key:p} pastes the last search pattern as text. Combined with {key::}{key:s}, you can reuse a complex pattern: {key::}{key:s}{key:/}{key:Ctrl-R}{key:/}{key:/}{replacement}{key:/}{key:g}.

blocks.title: Special registers are control channels
blocks.text: {key:"}{key:/} reads/writes the search pattern. {key:"}{key:=} prompts for a Vim-script expression and yields its result on read. {key:"}{key:_} discards anything written to it and yields nothing on read. They share the register interface but have nothing in common with regular storage — they're protocol endpoints disguised as registers.
